page: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-8g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for massive parallel computing workloads. It features eight NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with a combined 640 GB of VRAM. The host system is powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor providing 96 dedicated vCPUs (48 physical cores with hyper-threading) running at 2.2 GHz, supported by 1360 GB of system memory. Local storage consists of 3216 GB of NVMe SSD capacity spread across eight local drives.

Benchmark data indicates highly polarized performance characteristics. Single-core CPU metrics are relatively weak, as shown by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests. However, multi-core and parallel execution capabilities are strong, with high multi-core stress-ng scores and strong performance in extended instruction sets like AVX-512. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high for read operations, reaching 4212.24 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 192.49 GB/sec for large blocks. Large block write bandwidth is average at 84.61 GB/sec.

The primary strength of this instance lies in its GPU-accelerated capabilities. In LLM inference benchmarks, the server achieves top-tier performance. For prompt processing, it delivers 6558.71 tokens/sec on a small 135M model, 2778.15 tokens/sec on a medium 7B model, and 410.88 tokens/sec on a large 70B model. Text generation speeds are also top-tier for larger models, reaching 110.69 tokens/sec on the 7B model and 20.62 tokens/sec on the 70B model.

Given the high density of premium hardware components, including eight enterprise GPUs and over a terabyte of RAM, this server represents a high-cost, high-resource tier. It is optimized for workloads where GPU and memory capacity are the primary bottlenecks rather than single-core CPU speed. Appropriate workloads include large-scale LLM training and inference, deep learning, high-performance computing, and complex data science pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-8g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for high-performance parallel computing. It features eight NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs delivering a total of 640 GB of VRAM, paired with 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1360 GB of system memory. Storage is supported by 3216 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity. While single-core CPU performance is relatively weak, the server excels in multi-core execution and memory read bandwidth, which reaches up to 4212.24 GB/sec. The hardware configuration is highly optimized for GPU-intensive tasks, demonstrating top-tier performance in LLM inference benchmarks across small, medium, and large models. This server is best suited for large-scale machine learning training, LLM inference, and complex data science workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-8g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 8 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1360 GB of RAM, and 3216 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-performance parallel computing.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-8g features 8 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1360 GB of RAM, and 3216 GB of NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized platform featuring eight enterprise GPUs and massive memory capacity for demanding machine learning and parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: Eight NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 640 GB total VRAM
96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1360 GB of system memory
3216 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier LLM inference performance for prompt processing and text generation
Strong multi-core processing and high-bandwidth memory read capabilities
Designed for deep learning, LLM training, and high-performance computing
